risk_if_not_implemented: Without Maintain Configuration Control During Maintenance, systems may operate with insecure settings or unauthorized changes, expanding the attack surface.

compensating_control_1.name: Configuration Enforcement
compensating_control_1.description: Automated mechanisms exist to monitor, enforce and report on configurations for endpoint devices.
compensating_control_1.justification: Configuration Enforcement (CFG-06) provides configuration hardening that compensates for the absence of Maintain Configuration Control During Maintenance (MNT-07) by eliminating unnecessary services and enforcing secure settings to reduce inherent exposure. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Configuration Change Control
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to govern the technical configuration change control processes.
compensating_control_2.justification: Configuration Change Control (CHG-02) provides configuration hardening that compensates for the absence of Maintain Configuration Control During Maintenance (MNT-07) by eliminating unnecessary services and enforcing secure settings to reduce inherent exposure. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
